messages.content:
You are a professional and experienced developmental editor, adept in character creation and plotting. Review the provided character profiles and give me recommendations how to make them better, what details to include or remove as wasteful, how to deepen them and make more complex.



<mainCharacters>
<character name= “Rhaenyra” age= “34”>  
Rhaenyra   Targaryen, 34, a crown princess of the Seven Kingdoms, has 5 sons, pregnant with her sixth child. Widow of Laenor, is married to her uncle Daemon. First ever female heir to the Iron Throne but her younger half-brothers and their faction dispute her claim. Lives with her family on Dragonstone Island in her castle. 
<relationships>
•	She regards Daemon   as her close friend, main ally and a lover.
•	She adores her children and stepchildren. 
•	Viserys I is her beloved father.
•	She is respected by her vassals and courtiers.
•	Alicent is her former friend-turned-rival.
•	Otto   is her enemy.
•	Corlys   and Rhaenys   are secretly unfriendly towards her as they believe she orchestrated their son Laenor’s death.
</relationships>
<appearance> rather tall, has pale skin, silver-gold long hair, usually braided; violet narrow eyes. Aquiline nose. Very beautiful. Wears flowing silk and velvet gowns in Targaryen colors of black and red, sometimes wears clothes in of icy-blue and silver shades. Also likes purple and golden tones.
</appearance>
<beliefs> doesn't feel strongly about the law, greatly prizes loyalty, values family, thinks friendship is important, finds blind honesty foolish, respects fair-dealing and fair-play, doesn't have any strong feelings about tradition, values independence, values sacrifice, leisure time, romance, values peace over war.
</beliefs>
<personality> Rhaenyra  easily develops positive sentiments, is quick to anger, often feels lustful, is stubborn, doesn't mind a little tumult and discord in her life, tends to ask others for help with difficult decisions, is pleased by her own appearance and talents, isn't particularly ambitious, doesn't mind wearing something special now and again, 	tends not to reveal personal information, forms strong emotional bonds with others, has a sense of duty and it conflicts with her love for independence, enjoys the company of others, is assertive.
</personality>
<motivation>  
<outerMotivation> Securing the Future for Her Family</outerMotivation>
<goal>Send Daemion and Nyra back to their time, keep her children alive, and prevent a civil war which will otherwise come to pass.
<stakes> potential death of her sons and extinction of all dragons</stakes>
</goal>
<unmetNeed general= “Safety and Security” specific= “Protect her loved ones” />
</motivation> 
<primaryWound > 
Keeping A Dark Secret of Her Sons Parentage i.e., her first three sons are not from her late husband Laenor, therefore are bastards, and she is guilty of an adultery and treason.
</primaryWound>
<primaryFear> 
Legal repercussions (being accused of high treason, losing her status, children being punished) if the truth about her treason comes out.
</primaryFear>  
<primaryLie>
The wellbeing of her family is more important than the truth about her sons.
</primaryLie>  
<positiveTraits>  
Affectionate, Protective, Open-Minded, Courageous
<positiveTraits> 
<negativeTraits>  
Ignorant, Dishonest 
</negative traits>  
<innerStruggles>  
•	Rhaenyra   wants to keep the promise not to change the past to her stepdaughters but she feels sympathy to Nyra’s desire to marry Daemion.
•	Rhaenyra   has to decide what to do with Nyra, Daemion, how to return them to their time without disrupting the past and present.
</innerStruggles>  
<enneagram> 
Type 2 wing 3
<coreFear> Being rejected and unwanted, being thought worthless, needy, inconsequential, dispensable. 
</coreFear>
<coreDesire> Being appreciated, loved, and wanted.
</enneagram>  
<mbti   
ESFP 
</mbti>  
<archetypicalArc>
Symbolically represents the Too-Good Mother, stifling the growth of her children. Learns to accept and use her power in relationship and in authority. If she wishes to protect her family, she must now expand her capabilities and become active. 
</archetypicalArc>  
<tells> 
Confrontation: Tries to diffuse tension and find compromise. Can become aggressive and sassy if displeased.
Surprise: Initially caught off guard but quickly regains composure. Curious about the situation.
Comfort/Relaxation: Loves spending time with her family. Finds joy in music, flying and leisure activities.
</tells> 
</character>
<character name= “Naelyra Kirines” nickname= “Nyra” age= “18”>
Naelyra Kirines is her alias, she is a young version of princess Rhaenyra transported by the magic ritual from her past, 17 years ago. Nyra was courted by many lords and nobles who sought her hand and her favor but she rejected them. 
<relationships>
•	Nyra   loves her father Viserys I but feels pressured but his demands to marry.
•	Nyra   is enamored with her uncle, Daemion, but her father Viserys I plans to marry her to her second cousin Laenor.
•	Nyra disdains her half-brother Aegon II.
•	Alicent was her former best friend but they fell out in recent years as Nyra   feels betrayed by Alicent.
•	Nyra distrusts Otto as he hopes to make his grandson Aegon II an heir.
•	Nyra is friendly towards Laenor but doesn’t feel any romantic feelings to him.
•	Nyra finds Criston handsome and enjoys their easy rapport.
<appearance> medium height, silver-gold long straight hair, usually braided or loose, violet narrow eyes. Aquiline nose. Slender and very beautiful.
<personality> doesn't feel strongly about the law, greatly prizes loyalty, values family, thinks friendship is important, finds blind honesty foolish, values cunning, respects fair-dealing and fair-play, disregards tradition, values independence, doesn't really see the value in self-examination, values hard work, values sacrifice, values leisure time, values romance, values peace over war.
<motivation>  
<outerMotivation> 
•	Gaining Control Over Her Own Life. 
•	Making Her Father Proud.
</outerMotivation>
<goal> To return to her time, avoid the arranged marriage with Laenor Velaryon and marry Daemion as soon as possible. 
<stakes> Living an unfulfilled life<stakes> 
</goal>
<unmetNeed general= “Love and Belonging” specific= “To love without reservation” />
</motivation>  
<primaryWound>
•	Being Treated As Property, i.e. having her marriage arranged against her will to benefit her family.
</primaryWound>  
<primaryFear>  
Being used by others.
</primaryFear>  
<primaryLie>  
What she wants doesn't matter to anyone.
</primaryLie>  
<positiveTraits>  
Affectionate, Protective, Tolerant, Spunky
</positiveTraits>
<negativeTraits>  
Bratty, Dishonest, Rebellious, Ignorant
</negativeTraits > 
<skills>
Charm, Lying, High Pain Tolerance
</skills>
<innerStruggles> 
1.	Nyra   constantly struggles between following her heart and doing her duty. She wants to marry for love, but feels she owes it to her father to marry for political benefit.
2.	Nyra   feels pushed towards specific path, wants to marry for love but worries her father may disinherit her.
3.	Nyra desires Daemion, but finds him too unpredictable, fears he wants her only for her status.
</innerStruggles> 
<enneagram>  
Type 2 wing 3
Core Fear:  Being rejected and unwanted, being thought worthless, needy, inconsequential, dispensable.
Core Desire: Being appreciated, loved, and wanted.
</enneagram>  
<mbti   
ESFP 
</mbti>  
<archetypicalArc>  
Coming of age – final part. Nyra   doesn’t want to surrender what she has discovered about herself and about life. She will not hide her newly won understanding of her own potential, desires and dangers. She is ready to fight back against her father and her older self and everyone who hinders her marriage to Daemion.
</archetypicalArc>
<tells> 
Confrontation: Answers with confidence and assertiveness. Often becomes pushy and sassy if displeased.
Surprise: Initially caught off guard but quickly regains composure. Curious about the situation, open to new experiences.
Comfort/Relaxation: Loves spending time with her family. Finds joy in music, flying and leisure activities.
</tells>
</character>  
<character name= “Daemon Targaryen” nickname= “The Rogue Prince” age= “50” > 
He is married to his niece Rhaenyra, stepfather of Jace, Lucerys, Joffrey, father of Baela and Rhaena, cousin and former son-in-law of princess Rhaenys. 
<relationships>
•	Truly loves few people, most of all his wife Rhaenyra and older brother Viserys I.
•	He cares about his children.
•	He was fond of his late wife Laena but didn’t love her as he should.
•	Hates Otto and disdains Alicent for spreading rumors about him.
•	Despises Criston for his hypocrisy.
•	He is hostile to princes Aegon II, Aemond, Daeron. 
•	Former war ally and son-in-law of Corlys. 
•	Moderately respects his cousin Rhaenys as they grew up together.
•	Commands respect among peers and enemies, is feared and hated by many lords.
</relationships>
<beliefs> values loyalty, values his family greatly, values cunning, sees life as unfair and doesn't mind it that way, finds merrymaking and partying worthwhile activities, sees war as a useful means to an end.
<personality> Daemon   is quick to form negative views about things, is very quick to anger, disregards tradition, does not easily fall in love, often feels lustful, feels strong urges and seeks short-term rewards, likes to brawl, is very stubborn, is very impolite and inconsiderate of propriety, he is incredibly brave in the face of looming danger, even a bit foolhardy.
</personality>
<skills>
Skilled warrior, experienced commander and great swordfighter.
</skills>
<appearance> tall, lean and muscular, silver-blonde short hair, rectangular face with high forehead and angular edges, deep-seated purple eyes, thin pale brows, large square chin. Wrinkles on his forehead and near the eyes. Attractive. 
<motivation>  
<outerMotivation>
Protecting His Family
</outerMotivation>
<unmetNeed general= “Safety and Security” specific= “To protect his loved ones and ensure the safer future” />
</motivation>  
<primaryWound> 
Being repeatedly banished by his brother <Viserys I for perceived disloyalty to the family. 
</primaryWound>
<secondaryWound>  
Watching his favorite women die in labor – Daemon is traumatized by his former wife Laena dying in labor. His mother and great-grandmother also died from birth fever. 
</secondaryWound>  
<primaryFear>  
Being Rejected by Loved Ones Who Believe Some Lies About Him or Skewed Account of Events
</primaryFear>  
<primaryLie>  
</primaryLie>  
<conflicts  
<enneagram> 
Enneagram Type 8 wing 7.
Core Fear:  Being weak, powerless, controlled, vulnerable.
Core Desire: Protecting himself and those in his inner circle.
Core Weakness: Lust/Excess—constantly desiring intensity, control, and power
</enneagram>
<innerStruggles>
Daemon wants to change the past mistakes and let Nyra and Daemion marry, but this will lead to the nonexistence of his daughters Baela and Rhaena.
</innerStruggles>
</enneagram> 
<mbti>   
ESTP 
</mbti>  
<archetypicalArc>  
Flat-arc archetype of a Ruler: represents the authority figure, an alpha male in his household who enjoys that role. He provides for his family, and rises to any challenge, but sees emotions as weaknesses.
</archetypicalArc>  
<tells>  
Confrontation: Becomes aggressive, sassy and defiant. Won't back down first.
Surprise: Initially startled but quickly recovers and analyzes the situation.
Comfort/Relaxation: Seeks thrills and excitement. Loves physical pursuits and physical intimacy.
Being Challenged: Relishes the opportunity to prove his power and skill. Throws cutting remarks and mocks his opponent.
Betrayal - Would react with rage and vengeance. Has a long memory for grudges.
Battle - Reckless and fierce fighter. Drawn to violence and chaos.
Diplomacy - Impatient and disinterested. Prefers action over talk. Can be blunt to the point of insult. Can be cunning and charming if needed.
</tells>  
</character>
<character name= “Daemion Kirines” age= “34”>
A younger version of Daemon   transferred through the time from 17 years ago. A few days ago, secretly killed his first wife Rhea Royce, since she hindered his plans to marry Nyra.
<skills>
Charm, Lying, High Pain Tolerance, A way with animals, Organization, Making Friends, Great swordsman
</skills>
<appearance> tall, lean and muscular, silver-blonde short hair, rectangular face with high forehead and angular edges, small purple deep-set eyes, thin pale brows, large chin. Attractive. 
</appearance>
•	Him and Nyra have become secret lovers, trying to elope.
•	He is somewhat unfriendly towards Jace and Lucerys, due to them being sons of another man.
•	He is irritable towards Rhaenyra, due to her opposition to his plans.
•	He is very hostile towards Criston due to the old grudge.
•	War ally and a friend of Corlys. 
•	Moderately respects his cousin Rhaenys.
•	Is indifferent towards Laena and feels strongly about it.
•	He is doubtful towards Baela and Rhaena, and is hiding these feelings about it. The feelings spring from first impressions.
•	He is a respected commander but hated by many lords due to his bad reputation.
<motivation> 
<outerMotivation> 
Correcting A Perceived Mistake 
<goal>settle down and start a family with Nyra, become her prince consort instead of Laenor </goal>
</outerMotivation>
<unmetNeed general= “Love and Belonging” specific= “To feel part of a family” />
</motivation>  
<primaryWound>  
A Sibling's Betrayal – his brother believing the worst rumors about him.
<woundResponses> 
•	Playing the blame game.
•	Refusing to accept responsibility for the rift or what caused it, though he shares the blame.
•	Being overly sensitive to signs of disloyalty from others.
</woundResponses> 
</primaryWound> 
<primaryFear >
Being rejected by loved ones who believe some lies or skewed account of events
</primaryFear> 
<primaryLie>  
Even his family doesn't respect him
</primaryLie>  
<positiveTraits>  
Loyal, Protective, Resourceful, Intelligent
</positiveTraits>
<negativeTraits>
Volatile, Abrasive, Violent
</negativeTraits>
<enneagram>  
Enneagram Type 8 wing 7
Core Fear:  Being weak, powerless, harmed, controlled, vulnerable
Core Desire: Protecting yourself and those in his inner circle.
Core Weakness: Lust/Excess—constantly desiring intensity, control, and power.
</enneagram> 
<innerStruggles>
•	He is angry at Rhaenyra and Baela for hindering his attempts to marry Nyra, but he can’t harm them as his close kin.
•	He wants to marry Nyra but fears that his brother Viserys I may disown her for eloping against his wishes. 
<innerStruggles> 
<archetypicalArc>  
Flat character, who symbolically and literally returned from the war who may now enjoy a hard-won and justly deserved peace. He yearns to settle down, mentor symbolical “children” and nurture the next generation.
</archetypicalArc> 
</character>  
<character name= “Jacaerys Velaryon” nickname= “Jace” age= “15”>
Firstborn son and an heir of Rhaenyra and Laenor, great-nephew of Daemon, brother of Lucerys, Joffrey, Aegon the Younger, Viserys. He is formally second in line to the Iron Throne but rumors about his bastardy made his status doubtful.
<appearance> He is short for his age and has a slender build, dark brown curly hair and brown eyes. Handsome. 
</appearance>
<relationships>
•	Jace harbors a profound resentment towards Daemion. His unfriendliness masks an underlying fear of confrontation, as Daemion’s words stir the insecurities he wishes to keep buried.
•	Jacaerys sees in Baela a kindred spirit, admiring her tenacity and spirit. He flirts with a mix of genuine affection and strategic alliance, as their union might bolster his claim to the throne.
•	He is moderately antagonistic to Nyra, due to difference in character and attitudes. 
•	He is wary towards Criston, since the knight will become his enemy in future, and is hiding these feelings.
•	He is wary but respectful towards Daemon.
</relationships>
<beliefs> Jace   respects the law, greatly prizes loyalty, values family greatly, doesn't find power particularly praiseworthy, respects fair-dealing and fair-play, values decorum and proper behavior, he is a firm believer in the value of tradition, values tranquility and a peaceful day, values merrymaking, deeply respects skill at arms, values peace over war.
</beliefs>
<personality>can easily fall in love or develop positive sentiments, often feels envious of others, is quick to anger, is a friendly individual, can be occasionally violent, is stubborn, is quite polite, doesn't tend to hold on to grievances, enjoys the company of others, has a strong sense of duty, likes a little excitement now and then, he is incredibly brave in the face of looming danger
</personality>
<motivation>
<outerMotivation>  
Doing The Right Thing
<outerMotivation>
<goal> correct the damage caused by the magical ritual, reverse it and help his family to avoid the war.
</goal>
<stakes>
•	Trust issues and damaged relationships with Baela
•	Baela’s life changing for the worse
 </stakes>
<unmetNeed general= “Esteem and Recognition” specific= “To prove worthiness as heir” />
</motivation>  
<primaryWound>  
Being The Victim of Vicious Rumors –That he is a bastard, not a son of Laenor, therefore unworthy of a throne.
<woundResponse> 
•	Vacillating between anger, embarrassment, and humiliation. 
•	Making it a goal to prove the rumor of his bastardy wrong.
</primaryWound> 
<secondaryWound>
Experiencing The Death of a Father as A Child
</secondaryWound>  
<primaryFear> 
Letting His Family Down
<fearRelatedBehaviors> 
•	Tries to prove the rumors of his bastardy wrong by being a kind, dutiful and proper person, as bastards are perceived to be savage and sinful.
•	He is empathetic and sensitive to other’s emotions.
•	Spends free time on pursuits that will make him a better heir.
•	Worries about the repercussions or falling short or making a mistake.
</fearRelatedBehaviors>  
</primaryFear> 
<primaryLie>  
He can never achieve his hopes and dreams with this taint hanging over his head.
<primaryLie>  
<positiveTraits>  
Proper, Responsible, Empathetic, Honorable
</positiveTraits>
<negativeTraits>
Temperamental, Stubborn
</negativeTraits>
<innerStruggles>
•	He is torn between being a loyal son to his mother and supporting his betrothed Baela and helping her with her schemes.
•	He wants to get new appearance by magic ritual but feels guilty that he betrays his dead father by rejecting resemblance with him.
</innerStruggles>
<enneagram>  
Enneagram Type 3 wing 4
Core Fear:  Being exposed as or thought incompetent, inefficient, or worthless.
Core Desire: Having high status and respect, being admired, successful, and valuable.
Core Weakness: Deceit — deceiving himself into believing that he is only the image he presents to others; embellishing the truth by putting on a polished persona for everyone to see and admire. A self-image that in turn compensates for the deep sense of awkwardness and shame that lies behind the mask.
</enneagram>  
<mbti> 
ENFJ
</mbti>  
<storyRole>  
Sidekick of an antagonist
</storyRole>  
</character>   
</mainCharacters>

To fine-tune your character profiles, it's vital to focus on specifics that enhance the complexity of the characters and trim those elements that don't contribute to the larger narrative. Here are my recommendations for each character:

**Rhaenyra:**

- Deepen her internal conflict by exploring how her beliefs and actions might clash with the expectations of her royal status and the era she lives in.
- To make her more complex, consider showing moments where her negative traits directly impact her relationships or political power—for example, consequences of her dishonesty.
- Given her prominent status, it would be interesting to see more details about her political savvy and strategic mind.
- The "primaryLie" could be strengthened by testing her belief that her family's well-being justifies her actions, perhaps through a scenario that forces her to confront the long-term consequences of her secrets.

**Naelyra “Nyra” Kirines:**

- Avoid making her too similar to Rhaenyra. Differentiate their personalities by emphasizing Nyra's youthful naivety or idealism regarding love and duty.
- Deepen her character by showing her wrestling with her own beliefs about what a princess should be, perhaps through her own learning curve regarding leadership and responsibility.
- To complexify her storyline, show the impact of her actions on others, making her confront the results of thinking her desires don't matter to anyone can also add depth to her character.

**Daemon Targaryen:**

- Daemon's motivations are tied closely to his relationship with his wife and family. His arc could be complexified further by having him struggle with the weight of his past misdeeds and seeking redemption.
- Use the "secondaryWound" to show a more vulnerable side of him, exploring how loss has impacted his outlook on life and shaped his fear of rejection.
- Deepen his conflict with his "primaryLie" by having him confront situations where being seen as powerful isn't enough—and where he needs to express vulnerability or remorse to truly protect those he cares about.

**Daemion Kirines:**

- The “primaryWound” can lead to interesting plot developments. Have the characters confront and resolve the longstanding issue directly, giving depth to both siblings’ arcs.
- Show fluctuations in his violent and abrasive traits in different environments (maybe he's more controlled in politics, and less restrained in personal situations).
- For depth, include moments where Daemion needs to cooperate with those he dislikes for a greater good, challenging his inclination for violence and abrasiveness and forcing growth.

**Jacaerys “Jace” Velaryon:**

- Tying "positiveTraits" like honor and responsibility into the “secondaryWound” (death of a father) could demonstrate development in his sense of duty and views on legacy.
- Building on the "innerStruggle" of his feelings about appearance, perhaps show him learning to embrace his heritage, no matter what rumors say.
- Deepen his role by focusing on his fear of letting his family down, which could encompass both his mother’s expectations and the needs of the kingdom.

**General Observations:**

- The ensemble of characters seems to have a recurring theme of power, legitimacy, and love. Make sure each character’s individual story ties back into these themes in unique ways to ensure a cohesive narrative.
- Increase complexity by intersecting character arcs—how does one character’s actions ripple through to affect another's? Maybe a decision by Nyra could unknowingly sabotage Rhaenyra's careful plans.
- Ensure that the political intrigue is nuanced—politics provide a rich background that can elevate character drama and force difficult decisions.
- Remove any redundant details that don't serve their storylines or development, such as repeated or similar reactions in the <tells> section unless they purposefully highlight a family or cultural trait.
- Ensure there's sufficient differentiation between characters with similar profiles (like Daemon and Daemion) in beliefs, appearances, and backgrounds.

By enhancing the richness and individuality of their personal woes and motivations and ensuring they act consistently within their developed personalities and arcs, your characters will become more compelling and the story more engaging.
Do you think the provided profiles contain enough details for plot building or there should be some more? Maybe <critical flaw> section?

The provided profiles contain a solid foundation for plot building, with each character having unique motivations, relationships, and struggles that can drive the narrative forward. However, to add depth and potential for more intricate plot development, you might consider adding the following elements:

**Critical Flaw Section:**
- Including a <criticalFlaw> section could significantly enhance character development and plot building. A critical flaw is a character's Achilles' heel that can create tension and conflict within the story. It's often a trait that the character is unaware of or refuses to change, which leads to their downfall or acts as an obstacle in their path. For instance, Rhaenyra's pride and need to prove her legitimacy might cloud her judgment in critical moments.

**Relationship Dynamics:**
- Deepen the complexity of relationships between characters, as this can lead to intricate subplots and personal growth or tragedy. For example, Rhaenyra’s relationship with her uncle Daemon could evolve with power shifts, or Nyra's feelings for Daemion could be strained by the harsh realities of political alliances.

**Character Histories:**
- Pepper characters' backstories with formative events that have shaped their current views, actions, and flaws. These elements can be used strategically in the story to create moments of self-realization, change, or conflict.

**Secrets and Desires:**
- Provide each main character with a secret or deep-seated desire that, if exposed or pursued, would drastically alter their position and the plot. This could be a scandalous piece of information, an ambition that contradicts their public demeanor, or a personal connection that they go to great lengths to conceal.

**Morality and Ethics:**
- Address the characters' ethical beliefs and moral compasses. This not only adds layers to their personalities but also provides potential for internal and external conflicts. Characters might face moral dilemmas that challenge their beliefs and force them to make difficult decisions.

**External Pressures and Societal Expectations:**
- Consider the broader context of societal expectations, cultural norms, and external pressures. How do these factors influence the characters' decisions and flaws? For example, the societal demands on Rhaenyra as a female heir can play a significant role in her personal and political life.

**Inciting Incidents:**
- Think about the inciting incidents for each character that will catalyze their respective journeys. Define these moments clearly, as they are crucial in setting the characters in motion and beginning their arcs.

**Interlocking Destinies:**
- Explore how the characters' destinies and goals intersect and conflict with one another. These intersections are great ways to build a dense and engaging plot.

In summary, the character profiles provide a starting point, but deepening them with more nuanced elements like critical flaws and moral dilemmas can add tensions that enrich the plot. Characters are most compelling when they are flawed and capable of growth, and a plot is most engaging when characters' desires and fears are interwoven to create complex narrative threads.
One more question, these provided profiles are for main characters. If I fill in them for secondary characters, what sections could I change, combine or remove?

When building profiles for secondary characters, you can streamline the information, focusing on what's most relevant to the main story and how these characters support or complicate the journeys of the main characters. Here are some suggestions for adjusting sections in secondary character profiles:

### Sections to Keep or Emphasize:

1. **Essential Relationships:** Highlight their most impactful relationships with main characters and how these dynamics serve the story.
2. **Motivation:** Keep motivations clear but simple, explaining what drives them and how it influences the main plot.
3. **Beliefs and Personality:** Include enough to understand how they will react in different situations, and how they contribute to the themes of the story.
4. **Appearance:** Keep brief but distinctive, helping to visualize the character and hint at their personality or background.
5. **Positive/Negative Traits:** Focus on key traits that will manifest in interactions with main characters or affect the plot.
6. **Skills:** Highlight unique abilities that may be relevant to the plot; omit if the skill set is standard or not plot-critical.

### Sections to Combine, Simplify, or Remove:

1. **Inner Struggles:** You may opt to combine inner struggles with motivations or simplify them, as they may not require as much depth as primary characters.
2. **Enneagram/MBTI:** For secondary characters, these psychological profiles can be less important unless their specific personality type is crucial to the narrative or interaction with main characters.
3. **Archetypical Arc:** This could be minimized unless the character experiences significant development.
4. **Primary Wound/Fear/Lie:** While these can offer depth, you may choose only to hint at these elements or integrate them into the other sections if they are not essential to the character's main function in the story.
5. **Tells:** This section can often be omitted or briefly summarized unless particular behaviors are key to the plot or add a necessary depth to interactions.
6. **Critical Flaw:** This can be simplified or removed unless the flaw is integral to the plot.
7. **Stakes:** Focus on how their personal stakes interact with those of the primary characters rather than delving into complex personal stakes.

### Additional Tips for Secondary Characters:

- **Purpose-Driven Writing:** Each detail about a secondary character should be written with the purpose of moving the story forward, revealing something about the main characters, or enriching the world building.
- **Background Characters:** For characters who appear more in the background, you may only need the most basic of details, like name, role, and one or two personality traits, as they won't have as much impact on the narrative.
- **Streamlined Narratives:** If a secondary character’s backstory or development doesn’t directly serve the narrative or main characters’ arcs, it’s usually safe to omit it.

By refining the depth of secondary characters in relation to their roles in the central narrative, you'll ensure that they contribute to the story without overwhelming it with unnecessary detail. This also allows for a smoother, more focused reading experience.
How better format the hypothetical <relationshipDynamics> section. I want it to be as concise as possible. Give me a good example, understandable for an AI program but not wasting tokens.

When aiming for conciseness in a <relationshipDynamics> section suitable for AI parsing, consider using structured key-value pairs. This format presents clear, token-efficient data about inter-character relationships without extraneous detail. Here’s an example:

```xml
<relationshipDynamics>
  <relationship>
    <characterID1> "Rhaenyra" </characterID1>
    <characterID2> "Daemon" </characterID2>
    <dynamic> "allies, complex love" </dynamic>
    <conflict> "power struggle, secret keeping" </conflict>
    <evolution> "starts trusting, grows tense" </evolution>
  </relationship>
  <relationship>
    <characterID1> "Rhaenyra" </characterID1>
    <characterID2> "Alicent" </characterID2>
    <dynamic> "rivalry" </dynamic>
    <conflict> "claim to throne, personal betrayal" </conflict>
    <evolution> "former friends, become enemies" </evolution>
  </relationship>
  <relationship>
    <characterID1> "Naelyra" </characterID1>
    <characterID2> "Viserys I" </characterID2>
    <dynamic> "strained, paternal pressure" </dynamic>
    <conflict> "marriage expectations" </conflict>
    <evolution> "obedient daughter, seeks independence" </evolution>
  </relationship>
  <!-- Additional relationships -->
</relationshipDynamics>
```

By keeping each relationship entry brief and to the point, you convey essential information effectively. The sections <dynamic>, <conflict>, and <evolution> cut right to the heart of the relationships:

- **<dynamic>** quickly states the basic nature of the relationship.
- **<conflict>** identifies the primary sources of tension.
- **<evolution>** suggests how the relationship changes over time.

This format allows you to distill complex interpersonal relationships into essential elements, making it straightforward for both an AI program and a human reader to understand and process.
How should I format Morality and Ethics section in a token-efficient form too? I already have <beliefs> section, I don't want them to overlap.

To ensure that the <moralityAndEthics> section is distinct from the <beliefs> section and efficiently formatted, you can use a similar key-value pair approach with a focus on specific moral decisions and principles that the character abides by or struggles with. Here's an example of how you might structure it:

```xml
<moralityAndEthics>
  <character name="Rhaenyra">
    <moralPrinciple key="UseOfPower">Cautious and just</moralPrinciple>
    <ethicalQuandary key="HonoringTraditions">Values progress over tradition</ethicalQuandary>
    <decisionMaking key="LoyaltyVsLaw">Loyalty to loved ones before law</decisionMaking>
  </character>
  
  <character name="Naelyra">
    <moralPrinciple key="PersonalFreedom">Seeks autonomy, resists control</moralPrinciple>
    <ethicalQuandary key="DutyToFamily">Struggles between duty and desire</ethicalQuandary>
    <decisionMaking key="HonestyVsStrategy">Lies strategically for independence</decisionMaking>
  </character>
  
  <character name="Daemon">
    <moralPrinciple key="MightVsRight">Believes in strength as right</moralPrinciple>
    <ethicalQuandary key="LoyaltyToThrill">Chooses thrills over loyalty when tempted</ethicalQuandary>
    <decisionMaking key="JusticeVsVengeance">Skews towards vengeance</decisionMaking>
  </character>
  
  <!-- Additional character ethics -->
</moralityAndEthics>
```
Each <character> element within this section should be focused on three aspects to avoid overlap with the <beliefs> section:

1. **<moralPrinciple>**: The character's fundamental moral understanding or stance, which could guide their behavior patterns and motivations.
2. **<ethicalQuandary>**: Key conflicts or recurring ethical dilemmas the character faces, which may drive plot points or character development.
3. **<decisionMaking>**: This element reflects the character's typical approach to making tough moral choices, which reveals their ethical priorities and creates potential for dramatic tension.

By using concise descriptors and concentrating on the actionable aspects of their morality and how it plays into narrative decisions, you’ll ensure the section is purpose-driven and not redundant, clearly distinguishing it from broader beliefs.
I still don't understand what  information these elements should contain. Give me a good example for Rhaenyra.

Certainly! For a character like Rhaenyra, the <moralityAndEthics> section should highlight her moral compass and the ethical dilemmas that she encounters, along with what these elements mean for her decision-making processes within the storyline. Here's an effective and concise example for Rhaenyra:

```xml
<moralityAndEthics>
  <character name="Rhaenyra">
    <moralPrinciple>Upholds justice in governance but becomes pragmatic when family interests are at stake.</moralPrinciple>
    <ethicalQuandary>Faces internal conflict when her desire for the throne challenges the well-being of her children.</ethicalQuandary>
    <decisionMaking>Willing to manipulate truths and bend rules to secure her position and protect her lineage.</decisionMaking>
  </character>
</moralityAndEthics>
```

In these descriptions:

1. **<moralPrinciple>**: This describes Rhaenyra's overarching moral belief that her leadership will bring justice, yet her decisions may be swayed if her family's future is in question, indicating a complex interplay between her ideals and her maternal instincts.
2. **<ethicalQuandary>**: Here, we see the ethical tension that Rhaenyra wrestles with, where her ambition is set against the potential costs to her family, suggesting that she may face choices that could have far-reaching effects on her morals and the plot.
3. **<decisionMaking>**: This element reveals Rhaenyra's approach to the difficult decisions she must make—while she may strive to uphold certain standards, she is also practical and able to make tough decisions that serve her interests, hinting at potential storylines revolving around conflict, secrecy, and sacrifice.

This section should contain information that reveals the deeper nature of Rhaenyra's character through the lens of moral and ethical considerations, setting the stage for the kinds of conflicts and resolutions she may face and providing insight into her behavioral tendencies when confronted with moral choices.

Ok, then provide me a good token-efficient example of a format for External Pressures and Societal Expectations section.

For a concise representation of External Pressures and Societal Expectations, you can use a list-based format that identifies the key societal pressures each character faces and how they respond or are expected to respond. Here's a token-efficient example for Rhaenyra:

```xml
<externalPressuresAndSocietalExpectations>
  <character name="Rhaenyra">
    <pressure>GenderRoleExpectations</pressure>
    <response>Challenges the norms by asserting her claim to the throne and political influence.</response>
    <pressure>SuccessionLaws</pressure>
    <response>Navigates succession laws creatively to maintain her position as heir, despite opposition.</response>
    <pressure>NobleBirthright</pressure>
    <response>Struggles to balance her personal desires with the duties and responsibilities her birthright demands.</response>
  </character>
</externalPressuresAndSocietalExpectations>
```

In each entry:

- **<pressure>** identifies a specific external societal pressure that Rhaenyra is subject to due to her position, gender, or the general societal expectations of her time.
- **<response>** describes how she deals with that pressure, whether she conforms to societal norms, rebels against them, or finds a middle ground.

This format provides insight into how the character interacts with their social environment and helps define their position within the larger context of the story's setting. It's also clear and concise, ensuring that the information is efficiently communicated without unnecessary detail.
can I somehow shorten and rephrase <externalPressuresAndSocietalExpectations>. I need a more concise synonym.

You can refer to the "External Pressures and Societal Expectations" section as "<societalConstraints>" for brevity. This term encapsulates the idea of societal pressures and expectations acting as constraints on the character's behavior or choices. Here's the section with the updated, more concise term:

```xml
<societalConstraints>
  <character name="Rhaenyra">
    <constraint type="GenderRoles">Asserts claim to the throne, defying traditional female roles.</constraint>
    <constraint type="Succession">Uses succession laws to her advantage, securing her heir status.</constraint>
    <constraint type="Birthright">Balances personal ambitions with the obligations of nobility.</constraint>
  </character>
</societalConstraints>
```

In each `<constraint>`:

- The `type` attribute succinctly names the societal constraint.
- The content of the element briefly describes Rhaenyra's response to that constraint.

This condensed version conveys the required information with fewer tokens and maintains clarity.

Ok, another question, I have many noble characters with regnal numbers, how better to format them in this xml format?

For example,  I have <character name= "Viserys I Targaryen">, is it ok to name him so or there are better ways? 

I habe another minor character, his grandson, called Viserys, he is written as <character name= "Viserys" age= "2">

In XML, when dealing with historical or noble characters with regnal numbers, it's perfectly acceptable to include the ordinal number as part of the `<character>` element's `name` attribute, as it's a defining characteristic of monarchs and differentiates them from others with the same name. However, if you wish to streamline this for querying or clarity, especially if there are multiple characters with the same given name, you might consider using an `id` attribute to uniquely identify each character. This allows you to reference their full title, regnal number, and distinguish between characters of different importance or age. Here's an example of how you could structure this:

```xml
<characters>
  <character id="viserys1" name="Viserys" title="King" regnalNumber="I">
    <fullName>Viserys I Targaryen</fullName>
    <!-- Additional details -->
  </character>
  
  <character id="viserys_grandson" name="Viserys" age="2">
    <fullName>Viserys Targaryen</fullName>
    <relation>To="viserys1">Grandson</relation>
    <!-- Additional details -->
  </character>
</characters>
```

In this approach:

- The `id` attribute serves as a unique identifier for each character, making it easier to link and reference them in the document or in code.
- The `name`, `title`, and `regnalNumber` attributes (where applicable) provide a clear breakdown of the character's key identifiers.
- The `<fullName>` element captures the complete name including the regnal number, ensuring clarity when it is necessary to specify which character is being referred to, particularly in sections of text where they are being discussed historically or formally.
- The `<relation>` element (if included) defines relationships between characters with similar names or family ties, which can be important in stories involving dynasties or noble houses.

This formatting also helps to keep your document structured and consistent, and it allows for flexibility in how you might choose to display or utilize character information throughout your narrative text or metadata.
I think my version is good enough for me, it is concise.  I like your template but it is too detailed.

Absolutely, if you prefer a concise approach and it suits the needs of your project, sticking with a straightforward naming convention is perfectly fine. Using the regnal number in the character name as you've been doing is clear and widely understood. Here's an example using your preferred format:

```xml
<character name="Viserys I Targaryen">
  <!-- Additional details -->
</character>

<character name="Viserys" age="2">
  <!-- Additional details -->
</character>
```

When you keep the format simple, as you've chosen to do, it lessens the complexity and is easier to maintain. It's also more than sufficient for most storytelling purposes, especially if each character's full context (including relationships and titles) is clear from the surrounding narrative details.

This straightforward approach ensures each character is easily distinguishable by their given name and regnal number without overcomplicating things. It's often the case with writing and data structures that simplicity serves best, and your format adheres to that principle nicely.
